---
title: "Projects"
description: "Some of the projects I've worked on over the years, all publicly available on GitHub."
sidebar: false
toc: false
---

::: {.projects-list}

::: {.project-item}
[building-from-scratch](https://github.com/garg-aayush/building-from-scratch)

My ongoing repo where I build language models and related concepts from first principles. I've worked through seven sub-projects so far: a character-level GPT, a GPT-2 124M reimplementation with RoPE, a BPE tokenizer from scratch (with a Rust encoder for 3.7x speedup), LLM inference with KV cache and speculative decoding, supervised fine-tuning for reasoning and instruction following, expert iteration for self-improvement on math, and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
:::

::: {.project-item}
[cv-skills](https://github.com/garg-aayush/cv-skills)

I built this as a Claude Code plugin that provides seven skills for classical image processing: format conversion, SVG-to-raster, resize/crop/rotate, color adjustments, blur/sharpen/denoise, edge detection and segmentation, and image compositing with background removal.
:::

::: {.project-item}
[pytorch-pl-hydra-templates](https://github.com/garg-aayush/pytorch-pl-hydra-templates)

I put together four progressively more sophisticated PyTorch training templates using CIFAR-10: vanilla single-GPU, multi-GPU with DistributedDataParallel, PyTorch Lightning with TensorBoard, and PyTorch Lightning + Hydra for rapid experimentation with swappable models, optimizers, and configs.
:::

::: {.project-item}
[model-parallelism](https://github.com/garg-aayush/model-parallelism)

I explored how to pipeline models with skip connections (ResNets, UNets) across multiple GPUs using torchgpipe. Covers the `@skippable` decorator for stashing/popping tensors across partitions, automatic model balancing, and micro-batch tuning.
:::

::: {.project-item}
[research-papers](https://github.com/garg-aayush/research-papers)

My reading notes and summaries for deep learning papers organized by category. Covers diffusion models (DDPM, DDIM, ControlNet, SDXL, and more), Transformers (Attention Is All You Need), and GANs.
:::

::: {.project-item}
[spatial-alias-removal](https://github.com/garg-aayush/spatial-alias-removal)

I used deep learning super-resolution (SRCNN, EDSR, VDSR) to upscale seismic data by 2x in the spatial direction and remove spatial aliasing. Built during my time at TU Delft and University of Amsterdam.
:::

::: {.project-item}
[ComfyUI-Svg2Raster](https://github.com/garg-aayush/ComfyUI-Svg2Raster)

I wrote this ComfyUI custom node package for loading SVG files and rasterizing them into images with configurable width, scale, background color, and border. Uses CairoSVG under the hood.
:::

::: {.project-item}
[finance_dashboard_example](https://github.com/garg-aayush/finance_dashboard_example)

A sample Plotly Dash interactive dashboard I built for tracking stock price movement with simple moving average and candlestick pattern charts.
:::

::: {.project-item}
[tutorials](https://github.com/garg-aayush/tutorials)

A collection of various tutorials I wrote for my own use and to share with others.
:::

:::

## Gists

::: {.projects-list}

::: {.project-item}
[Step-by-Step Guide to setup your own personal GPU server](https://gist.github.com/garg-aayush/d377557b20ef9f206c1d6381e174d8a7)

I wrote this comprehensive guide for setting up remote SSH access to a personal GPU workstation running Ubuntu, covering SSH keys, firewall setup, and ngrok tunneling.
:::

::: {.project-item}
[Managing multiple CUDA versions using environment modules](https://gist.github.com/garg-aayush/156ec6ddda3d62e2c0ddad00b7e66956)

My notes on installing multiple CUDA versions side by side on Ubuntu and switching between them using `environment-modules`.
:::

:::
